source: CSIS / BBC-Mediazona / CIA estimate / Ukrainian MoD (Apr 26, 2026)
note: Russia has never released official casualty figures. Ukrainian MoD reported ~1,325,650 total personnel losses by Apr 26, 2026 — ~960 killed/wounded on Apr 26 alone; 255,862+ UAVs, 11,892+ tanks, 24,458+ AFVs, 40,635+ artillery systems destroyed. Mediazona/BBC confirmed 206,202 deaths by name as of Mar 30, 2026. Kremlin's own classified documents (per Ukrainian HUR) reportedly put KIA+WIA at 1,315,000. Daily losses remain extremely high. 80% of Russian personnel kills attributed to Ukrainian UAV operators.

source: Ukrainian MoD / CSIS Jan 2026 / Western officials (Apr 26, 2026)
note: Total personnel losses reach ~1,325,650 per Ukrainian MoD (April 26, 2026). Equipment: 11,892+ tanks, 24,458+ AFVs, 40,635+ artillery systems, 1,753+ MLRS, 1,353+ air defense assets, 435+ aircraft, 350+ helicopters, 255,862+ UAVs, 33+ warships. CSIS Jan 2026: ~1.2M. Russia losing more soldiers than it can recruit since Nov 2025 (~40K/month). Monthly loss rates most sustained since WWII. Russia's spring 2026 offensive driving elevated daily casualty rates with 149 combat engagements on Apr 26.

Oryx counts only losses confirmed by photo/video evidence. Ukrainian MoD claims are higher. Equipment losses represent most of Russia's pre-war stocks, replaced by reserve stocks and wartime production. Ukrainian drone units destroyed 6 BM-21 Grad launchers in Pokrovsk area in Mar 2026.

Zelenskyy stated 55,000 soldiers killed in 2026. UALosses project documented 86,142 by name. CSIS estimates 100K–140K KIA. Western officials (Feb 2026): 250K–300K total KIA+WIA. Ukrainian government has strict OPSEC around casualty figures.

Ukrainian losses significantly lower than Russian but still catastrophic for a country of 30M. Manpower shortages led to mobilization law in 2024 lowering conscription age to 25. Acute manpower shortages ongoing as of early 2026.

UN emphasizes verified figures are significant undercounts — particularly in Russian-occupied territory where documentation is impossible. 97% of 2025 civilian casualties in government-controlled areas attributed to Russian attacks. Russian aerial attack on Sloviansk killed 4 and injured 16+ in Mar 2026.
2022 was the deadliest year for civilians, particularly the opening months with siege warfare and mass atrocities in Bucha, Mariupol, and Kherson Oblast.
2025 was the deadliest year for civilians since 2022, with a 31% rise over 2024. Russia's intensified aerial campaign against civilian infrastructure drove the increase.

source: Mariupol City Council estimate / Kyiv Independent
note: Mariupol Mayor Boychenko estimated 22,000 civilians killed during siege. Ukrainian and Western officials consider this credible; Russia disputed. The actual figure may be higher — body recovery ongoing.
3.7 million Ukrainians internally displaced. 73% have been displaced for 2+ years. 10.8 million in need of humanitarian assistance in 2026.
5.86 million Ukrainian refugees recorded abroad, predominantly in Europe (~5.3M). Largest European refugee crisis since WWII. Germany, Poland, and Czech Republic host most refugees.

category: POWs — Ukrainian fighters held by Russia
note: Over 3,000 Ukrainian military POWs confirmed via ICRC. Azovstal garrison (2,439) taken prisoner May 2022. Russia accused of mistreating POWs; Ukraine requested ICRC access; access denied in many cases.

category: POWs — Russian soldiers held by Ukraine
note: Ukraine held over 5,000+ Russian POWs as of early 2025. Major prisoner exchange agreed at Istanbul talks (May 2025) — largest of the war, 1,000+ soldiers on each side. ICRC has access.

category: North Korean Troops — Casualties in Russia
note: South Korean intelligence estimated North Korea suffered extremely high losses during Kursk fighting — reportedly from lack of familiarity with drone warfare. North Korea denied casualties. ~11,000 troops total deployed.

Ukrainian authorities track child casualties specifically as evidence of war crimes. OHCHR confirms children among civilian casualties. 8 children were among 38 killed in Ternopil missile strike (Nov 2025).
